channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-06-26
item.description:
Our community has seen significant progress across multiple projects recently. The `scienceverse/metacheck` project has added a feature to flag p-values reported as exactly zero, while also rewriting the OSF prereg extraction to use a schema-driven dispatch, both of which are now part of the `dev` branch. Meanwhile, `giladfeldman/docpluck` has made targeted fixes to its tables, including addressing specific issues with DP-3 and DP-5. The `Davidvandijcke/coarse` project has made strides in localization and deployment, with updates to both the `main` and `dev` branches. Additionally, `JamieCummins/regcheck` has improved local reporting on its `staging` branch. These updates demonstrate the connections and collaborations within our community, with multiple projects focusing on enhancing accuracy, efficiency, and usability. Overall, it's an exciting time for our community, with many projects making notable progress and laying the groundwork for future advancements.

38 commits across 4 active repositories this week.

Dormant repos revived: escicheck, auto-zcurve

Most active:

docpluck15 commits
metacheck10 commits
regcheck10 commits
escicheck3 commits

item.title: metacheck: 2 commits today
item.description:
scienceverse/metacheck

The `dev` branch was the focus of recent work, where a merge occurred to add a feature that flags p-values reported as exactly zero, as indicated by the commit message "Merge exact_p_zero_warning: flag p-values reported as exactly zero". Additionally on the `dev` branch, the OSF prereg extraction was rewritten to use a schema-driven dispatch, as stated in the commit "Rewrite OSF prereg extraction to schema-driven dispatch". No other branches were worked on according to the provided commits. The exact_p_zero_warning feature is now part of the `dev` branch, allowing for the identification of p-values that are exactly zero. The schema-driven dispatch rewrite for OSF prereg extraction aims to enhance the efficiency or accuracy of this process on the `dev` branch.

2 commit(s) today:

  • 6937e9c [dev] Merge exact_p_zero_warning: flag p-values reported as exactly zero — Lakens
  • 3c854b3 [dev] Rewrite OSF prereg extraction to schema-driven dispatch — Lakens

View all commits →

item.title: docpluck: 1 commit today
item.description:
giladfeldman/docpluck

The `main` branch was worked on, where a fix was made to the tables, specifically addressing DP-3 and DP-5. The commit message mentions that the fix is for "η²p-from-structure" in DP-3 and "r-cell + bracket-CI" in DP-5. The fix also involves an "ESCIcheck handoff" and is noted to be "flatten-only" with "AI-gold-verified" results. This change is associated with version 2.4.98. The commit is a targeted fix, as indicated by the "fix(tables)" label.

1 commit(s) today:

  • b6bd65e [main] fix(tables): v2.4.98 — DP-3 η²p-from-structure, DP-5 r-cell + bracket-CI (ESCIcheck handoff; flatten-only, AI-gold-verified) — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: regcheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
JamieCummins/regcheck

The `staging` branch was worked on, where the commit message specifically mentions that local reporting was improved. The change made was to "improve local reporting", as stated in the commit message, but no further details are provided. No other branches were modified in this set of commits, with all changes being confined to the `staging` branch. The commit does not specify what exactly was improved in local reporting, only that it was the focus of the change. There are no additional features, fixes, or modules mentioned beyond the improvement to local reporting. No other work was done outside of the `staging` branch, according to the provided commit messages.

1 commit(s) today:

  • fdfb805 [staging] improve local reporting — Jamie Cummins

View all commits →

item.title: escicheck: activity update
item.description:
giladfeldman/escicheck

The `main` branch was updated with a commit that synchronized it with the `escicheckapp` repository at commit `f37b5a1`, which corresponds to `effectcheck` version `0.6.6`, as stated in the commit message "sync from escicheckapp@f37b5a1 (effectcheck v0.6.6)". This commit is associated with pull request `#20`. No other branches were worked on in this set of commits. The specific change made was to bring the `main` branch in line with the `escicheckapp` repository at the specified commit. The commit message does not detail any specific features or fixes added, only the synchronization with the other repository.

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →

item.title: auto-zcurve: activity update
item.description:
shaheedazaad/auto-zcurve

The `main` branch was worked on, where the validation note was revised and a citation was added, as stated in the commit message "Revise validation note and include citation". No other branches were modified in this set of commits. The revision of the validation note and the inclusion of a citation are the specific changes made in this commit. There are no new features or modules added in this set of commits, only the revision and addition of a citation. The commit message does not mention any fixes, so no bug fixes were made in this set of commits. The work was limited to a single commit on the `main` branch.

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →
